goal: Improve hub graph (surface check conflicts + shared-file edges) and agent guidance (mention hub, bump version)

summary.title: Improve hub graph (3 edge kinds) + agent guidance v8 mentions hub
summary.what: Two paired improvements driven by a thorough audit of two surfaces. Graph: extend `buildRelations` to emit four edge kinds (`supersedes`, `superseded_by`, `conflicts_with`, `shares_file`) instead of just supersessions. Add `HubCheckSummary` on `HubIntent` mirroring `domain.CheckSummary` so phase-2 check judgments persist into the Hub. Pre-group rows by kind in the renderer; render symmetric `shares_file` edges in one direction per pair; cap per-intent fan-out and require ≥2 shared files to suppress noise. Agent guidance: bump template to v8 and add a short section explaining when agents should suggest `mainline hub` to humans (file history, team activity, risk review) — the template predated hub entirely.
summary.why: Audit findings: (1) the graph page was empty on most repos because it only carried `SupersededByIntent`, while `LastCheck.AgainstIntents` was persisted but never exported; the implicit shared-file graph the conflict-detector already uses internally was invisible to the Hub. (2) Agent guidance v7 had zero mention of `mainline hub`, so agents had no idea to suggest it when humans asked file-history / risk-review questions that hub answers in one click. Both were the kind of low-cost-high-leverage fixes that justify a single review-and-improve PR.
summary.user_goal: Make the graph page useful on a typical repo (not just ones with supersessions) and make sure agents know hub exists so they recommend it when humans ask the questions hub is designed for.

decisions.rationale: Surfaces are ordered by how much the agent actively wrote: supersession is an explicit decision, conflicts_with is a judgment from `mainline check`, shares_file is purely implicit overlap. Strong-first ordering means the page leads with the signal that's most actionable.

decisions.chose: min 2-file overlap + cap 3 per intent + render only one direction (a < b) per pair.
decisions.rationale: On the dogfood repo, the unbounded version produced 496 rows. Min-overlap (drops single-file pairs that are mostly noise — every PR touches root.go), cap (bounds fan-out), one-direction (avoids redundant bidirectional rendering of a symmetric edge) bring it to 102 useful rows. The model JSON still carries both directions so a future API can answer either side cheaply.

decisions.chose: Carry has_conflict, highest_severity, against_intents, at — drop Evidence blob.
decisions.rationale: Hub v1 only needs the boolean + IDs to render edges; the full Evidence blob is large and re-derivable from `mainline show`/`mainline check`. Narrow contract here keeps Hub v2's API DTO lean.

decisions.point: agent guidance: where to put hub
decisions.chose: New section between 'Optional: agent hooks' and 'What you do NOT need to run', framed as 'you don't run this; you suggest it'.
decisions.rationale: Hub is a human-reader surface. Telling agents to RUN hub would be wrong — they have `context`/`show`/`trace` already. Telling them to SUGGEST it answers the real failure mode: agents that ignore hub when a human asks 'what's the history of this file?'.

decisions.chose: Assert against EmbeddedAgentsMDVersion() not the literal '7'.
decisions.rationale: Routine version bumps should not require touching upsertAgentsMD tests. Version-anchored assertions are a maintenance trap — the previous v8 bump immediately broke a test that hard-coded v7.

rejected.alternative: Drop `shares_file` until a real graph view is built.
rejected.reason: It's the only edge type that reliably has data on a fresh repo. Without it the page is back to 'empty for most users'. Bounded with min-overlap + cap, it's actionable enough.

Supersedes is asymmetric (A supersedes B is not symmetric to B). Conflicts_with is rendered both ways so each intent's page can show 'I conflict with X' independently. Shares_file is symmetric undirected — both rows tell the reader the same thing.

rejected.alternative: Add hub as a regular CLI for agents to run.
rejected.reason: Hub is a static-site renderer. Agents already have `context`/`show`/`trace` for programmatic access. Suggest-when-asked keeps the agent loop unchanged.

risks: shares_file weights are heuristic. min-overlap=2 + cap=3 chosen by inspection on one dogfood repo; may need tuning on bigger repos. Followup if a user reports the page is too noisy or too sparse.

Agent guidance v8 introduces an update_available state on every existing repo. Users who run `mainline agents check` will see migrate prompts; that's the system working as designed but does generate noise on first contact.
followups: Add a per-page intent header showing related/conflicts/shares_file edges alongside the existing supersession links — currently the new edges only appear on graph.html.
Consider tuning sharesFileCap and sharesFileMinOverlap as more repos dogfood the graph view.
If `mainline check --submit` becomes more common, surface the latest check's against_intents directly on the intent detail page.

architectural_claims: HubCheckSummary mirrors domain.CheckSummary but trimmed to the fields the v2 API DTO needs (has_conflict + against_intents + severity).
buildRelations is the single source of truth for intent-to-intent edges; the renderer only buckets and orders the rows it produces.
The agent guidance template is the contract for what agents are told about Mainline; new product surfaces (hub) require a template version bump, not a side-channel docs change.
behavioral_changes: Hub graph page now renders three edge kinds with per-kind sections; previously empty on repos without supersessions.
data/intents.json includes per-intent last_check + edge kind=conflicts_with/shares_file; Hub v2 API will inherit this shape.
Agent guidance template at v8; existing repos see update_available on `mainline agents check`.

api_changes.signature: hub.HubRelationRow.Kind now ranges over supersedes|superseded_by|conflicts_with|shares_file (was supersedes|superseded_by); gains optional Note

data_model_changes.name: Hub data/intents.json shape
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Additive: existing readers keep working; new fields (last_check, edge kinds, note) are optional.

data_model_changes.name: agents-md template version
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Bumped to v8. `mainline agents update` migrates in place; user content outside the markers is preserved.

migration_notes: Run `mainline agents update` to pick up the v8 template (adds the hub-suggestion section).
